reasoning: The current baseline reference period (360 days prior to 2026-04-12) had a monthly average of about 0.667 fatalities, meaning there were only ~8 fatalities in the entire year in Tunisia tracked by ACLED. The threshold for the question to resolve YES for any given 30-day period is 10 * (1 + 0.667) = 16.67, so it requires 17 or more fatalities in the 30 days preceding the resolution date. Historically, monthly fatalities exceeding 17 in Tunisia have been exceptionally rare since the major terrorist attacks in 2015 (Bardo, Sousse), with the base rate being ~1%. For the nearest dates (April and May 2026), there have been no recent major multi-fatality events matching this scale, so the probability is very low (0.02 - 0.03). For more distant dates (2029, 2031, 2036), the threshold remains anchored to the 2025-2026 period, creating a risk that if Tunisia’s political stability deteriorates and it descends into a more violent internal conflict, the 17+ fatality mark would be easily and consistently exceeded. Thus, the probability gradually scales up to reflect the long-tail structural risks of political violence or terrorism re-escalating over a 10-year horizon.
